findings.evidence: Line 109 declares `abbrev ofIsSmoothEmbedding`; the body at lines 110-112 exposes the chosen `ContMDiffMap` packaging.
findings.fix: Make `ofIsSmoothEmbedding` a `def` and keep the existing `@[simp]` characteristic lemmas as the public interface.
findings.issue: `ofIsSmoothEmbedding` is an `abbrev`, so the constructor body is reducible public API even though lines 115-120 already provide the needed coercion/apply lemmas.

findings.evidence: The new API has `id` at line 128 and `prodMap` at line 150, but `rg "SmoothEmbedding\.comp" code/TauCeti` finds no bundled composition; Mathlib has the predicate operation at `Mathlib/Geometry/Manifold/SmoothEmbedding.lean:113-116`.
findings.fix: Add `SmoothEmbedding.comp` with a `@[simp]` `comp_apply` lemma, using the predicate-level composition theorem.
findings.issue: The bundle exposes identity, open inclusion, product, and coproduct constructors but no bundled composition operation, so downstream users cannot compose smooth embeddings as bundled data without rebuilding via `ofIsSmoothEmbedding`.

summary: The bundled type is useful, but the public API exposes a reducible constructor body and omits the standard composition operation needed to use the bundle as first-class embedding data.
